# [enhancement] SDK-layer config file — migrate daemon/service-layer keys out of memory-core/config.template.mjs

## Context

PR #10102 config-lifted `guideGapWeightThreshold` into `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs` as the path of least resistance — `aiConfig` was already imported via `ai/services.mjs`, and the memory-core config has been the historical catch-all for cross-cutting knobs. Self-review caught the architectural mismatch: the threshold is consumed by `ai/daemons/services/GapInferenceEngine` (a daemon service), not by memory-core.

Per the mid-term architecture ("services out of MCP servers into the SDK, MCP servers as thin wrappers"), daemon/service-layer config keys should live in a dedicated SDK-layer config file, not inside any specific MCP server's config.

## The Problem

`memory-core/config.template.mjs` currently mixes two concerns:

1. **Memory-core-specific config**: Chroma paths, SQLite paths, summarization batch limits, auth (transport/port/realm), etc. These legitimately belong to the memory-core MCP server.
2. **Cross-cutting daemon/service config**: `autoDream`, `autoGoldenPath`, `autoIngestFileSystem`, `realTimeMemoryParsing`, `decayFactor`, `guideGapWeightThreshold`, `modelProvider`, `neoEmbeddingProvider`, `chromaEmbeddingProvider`, `handoffFilePath`, and similar. These govern daemon behavior / service defaults, not memory-core internals.

As services migrate out of MCP servers per the SDK consolidation plan, keeping daemon config inside a specific MCP server becomes increasingly awkward. New daemon config keys have no architecturally-correct home today.

## The Fix

1. Create `ai/config.mjs` (or `ai/services.config.mjs` — naming TBD) as the SDK-layer config destination.
2. Mirror the `Memory_Config` proxy pattern: default object + `Neo.core.Base` singleton + proxy for `aiConfig.data.X` access.
3. Identify the cross-cutting subset in `memory-core/config.template.mjs` — candidates above.
4. Migrate those keys to the SDK config. Update all consumer imports (`services.mjs` registration, `DreamService`, `GapInferenceEngine`, `ConceptIngestor`, `GoldenPathSynthesizer`, etc.).
5. Preserve env-var contracts (e.g., `NEO_GUIDE_GAP_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD` still works).
6. Update `bootstrapWorktree.mjs` if it needs to handle the new file.
7. Document the boundary in `learn/agentos/` so future config-lift decisions don't default to memory-core by convenience.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] New SDK-layer config file exists with default values + env-override pattern
- [ ] Cross-cutting daemon/service keys migrated (enumerate during implementation; at minimum: `guideGapWeightThreshold` + `decayFactor` + `autoDream` + `autoGoldenPath`)
- [ ] Memory-core config retains only memory-core-specific keys (Chroma, SQLite, auth, summarization, etc.)
- [ ] All consumers updated to import from the new SDK config
- [ ] Env-var names unchanged (operator contract preserved)
- [ ] `bootstrapWorktree.mjs` handles the new file if required
- [ ] `learn/agentos/` has a short doc explaining the MCP-config vs SDK-config boundary
- [ ] All existing tests pass; new smoke test verifies at least one SDK-config key reads correctly at runtime

## Out of Scope

- Renaming `guideGapWeightThreshold` → `conceptGapWeightThreshold` (separate semantic-cleanup ticket if desired).
- Migrating the `knowledge-base` or `github-workflow` server configs — this ticket is specifically about splitting memory-core's cross-cutting keys into the SDK layer.

## Related

- Parent epic: #10030 (Concept Ontology & Semantic Gap Inference) — indirect; surfaces here because #10086 exposed the placement gap.
- Origin PR: #10102 (self-review flagged this as the architectural destination for `guideGapWeightThreshold`).
- Downstream: future daemon/service config keys will have an architecturally-correct home.

## Durable Memory Entries

- `feedback_config_service_boundary.md` — validated 2026-04-19: config-lifts must match consuming service's ownership, not import-convenience.

## Timeline

- 2026-04-19T15:31:56Z @tobiu added the `enhancement` label

- 2026-04-19T15:32:11Z @tobiu cross-referenced by PR #10102
- 2026-04-19T15:39:36Z @tobiu cross-referenced by #10104
- 2026-04-19T16:26:28Z @tobiu referenced in commit `4df4b09` - "feat(ai): config-lift ConceptDiscoveryService tuning knobs (#10036)

Addresses tobi's polish catch on PR #10105: `DEFAULT_PR_SCAN_LIMIT = 20` and
`MIN_SOURCE_LENGTH = 200` were file-local magic numbers. Operator-tuning knobs
belong in config, not as consts buried in the service. Same discipline as
#10086 (`guideGapWeightThreshold` config-lift).

## Scope

- `config.template.mjs` + local `config.mjs`: new `conceptDiscovery` nested
  object grouping `prScanLimit` (default 20) + `minSourceLength` (default 200).
  Env overrides via `NEO_CONCEPT_DISCOVERY_PR_SCAN_LIMIT` +
  `NEO_CONCEPT_DISCOVERY_MIN_SOURCE_LENGTH`. Nested grouping matches existing
  `openAiCompatible` / `ollama` convention for service-scoped configs.
- `ConceptDiscoveryService.mjs`: dropped the two constants. Reads from
  `aiConfig.data.conceptDiscovery?.prScanLimit ?? 20` +
  `aiConfig.data.conceptDiscovery?.minSourceLength ?? 200` at call time (live
  reads, not module-load capture — enables runtime tuning + test overrides
  without reimport). Instance-level `prScanLimit` field retained for test
  overrides (falls through to aiConfig when null).
- Parity: local `config.mjs` mirrors the template addition (gitignored per
  `.gitignore`; not committed — only template goes into git).

## Out of scope (tracked for #10103)

Per `feedback_config_service_boundary.md` + #10103: these keys belong on the
SDK-layer config (ConceptDiscoveryService is a daemon service, not a memory-
core concern). Placed in memory-core config as the pragmatic destination today
— same debt as `guideGapWeightThreshold`. Migration is #10103's scope.

## Tests (49/49 stable × 3 runs)

New test: `aiConfig.data.conceptDiscovery.minSourceLength` override changes
extraction behavior. Proves the config is read at call time (raise threshold
above body length → LLM skipped; restore default → LLM invoked).

Origin Session ID: 1db25bbe-f39d-4dcd-bb0e-bc125ce91326"
- 2026-04-19T16:26:32Z @tobiu cross-referenced by PR #10105
- 2026-04-19T16:30:19Z @tobiu referenced in commit `a5802d7` - "feat(ai): ConceptDiscoveryService + validated flag for mined candidates (#10036) (#10105)

* feat(ai): ConceptDiscoveryService (LLM-based) + validated flag (#10036)

Lands deliverables 1, 2, and 4 of #10036 in one cohesive slice per the intake
discussion.

## Approach

LLM-driven concept extraction via `OpenAiCompatible.generate` (gemma4 or
configured provider — same substrate used by `SemanticGraphExtractor` and
`GoldenPathSynthesizer`). Two high-signal corpora:

- **Epics** — issue markdown files labeled `epic` in `resources/content/issues/`.
  Epic bodies are curated distilled architecture.
- **Pull requests** — `resources/content/pulls/*.md`, including their
  `## Comments` section. PR review comments contain `[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]` /
  `[RETROSPECTIVE]` / `[KB_GAP]` / "Gold Standards" / "Traps Avoided" prose —
  the exact architectural discourse where new vocabulary emerges. Sorted
  descending by PR number (freshest discourse first), capped at
  `DEFAULT_PR_SCAN_LIMIT = 20` per cycle to bound LLM cost.

The system prompt encodes the Teaching Test + explicit anti-patterns (class
names, file paths, framework names without architectural weight, routine
ticket vocabulary). Output is strict JSON parsed via `Json.extract` for
fence-tolerance. Per-source malformed output is logged and skipped — never
fatal to the cycle.

## Schema (Deliverable 4)

- `validated: boolean` field on concept nodes. Default `undefined` (legacy)
  treated as validated (no data migration). Mined candidates carry
  `validated: false`, tier 3, default weight → silenced in sandman_handoff.md
  via two independent mechanisms (weight gate + validated gate).
- `ConceptIngestor.computePayloadHash` includes validated, so flipping
  curator-review state triggers re-upsert.
- `ConceptIngestor` upsert-properties include validated, propagating to graph.
- `GapInferenceEngine.inferConceptGraphGaps` skips `validated === false`
  concepts regardless of weight — belt-and-suspenders alongside the weight
  gate. All three signals (GUIDE_GAP / EXAMPLE_GAP / ORPHAN_CONCEPT) respect
  the gate.
- Curator promotion = edit nodes.jsonl: flip `validated: true` + bump tier
  and/or weight. Git diff is the review surface.

## Tests (48/48 stable × 5 runs)

- `ConceptDiscoveryService.spec.mjs` (6 tests): epic-label filtering,
  PR cap + recency ordering, ConceptService dedupe (by id + alias),
  malformed-LLM tolerance, MIN_SOURCE_LENGTH short-circuit, end-to-end
  runDiscoveryCycle + JSONL append. Mocks `OpenAiCompatible.prototype.generate`
  with queued responses per call. Symmetric afterEach per
  `feedback_symmetric_spec_cleanup.md`.
- `DreamService.spec.mjs` new test: `validated: false` silences all three
  concept-graph signals even with weight 1.3 > 0.8 threshold.

## Deferred (out of this PR)

- **Deliverable 3** (KB-assisted classification) — blocked on KB MCP timeout
  per ticket body.
- **Deliverable 5** (glossary gap report) — pure reporting tool; file when
  ontology matures.
- **REM-cycle integration** — manual invocation via
  `DreamService.runConceptDiscovery()` for now. Per-cycle LLM cost would
  flood nodes.jsonl; deliberate to let curator/operator set cadence.
- **Session-summary mining** — epics + PRs are higher signal; add
  session-based mining as a follow-up if epic/PR coverage proves insufficient.

### @tobiu - 2026-04-20T20:04:04Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## Additional migration candidate: `backupPath`
> 
> Surfaced during #10132 / #10133 cleanup review on 2026-04-20.
> 
> **Current state:**
> - `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.mjs:205` + `.../config.template.mjs:272` define `backupPath: path.resolve(cwd, '.neo-ai-data/backups')`
> - `ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/config.mjs` + `...template.mjs` — **no equivalent entry**
> 
> **Consumers:**
> - `Memory_DatabaseService#exportDatabase` defaults `backupPath` to `aiConfig.backupPath` (MC config — works today)
> - `KB_DatabaseService#exportDatabase` defaults `backupPath` to `aiConfig.backupPath` (KB config — **undefined**; would throw at `fs.ensureDir(undefined)` if called without an explicit arg)
> - `buildScripts/ai/backup.mjs` orchestrator — always passes `backupPath` explicitly (one bundle subfolder per subsystem), so the KB default path is not exercised in practice today
> 
> **Why this belongs in #10103 rather than a new ticket:**
> - `backupPath` is a canonical cross-cutting filesystem root consumed by an operator script + two peer services — fits the "cross-cutting daemon/service config" profile in this ticket's Problem section
> - Fixing it in isolation (e.g. by adding `backupPath` to KB config too) doubles the debt rather than resolving it — the asymmetry just moves from "MC has it, KB doesn't" to "MC has one copy, KB has an identical copy"
> - When the SDK-layer config file lands per this ticket's Fix section, `backupPath` should migrate alongside the other cross-cutting keys (`guideGapWeightThreshold`, `decayFactor`, `autoDream`, etc.) — both MC and KB services then pull from the same SDK-level default
> 
> **Latent fragility note:** the KB default is currently `undefined` — safe only because `backup.mjs` always passes the arg. If a future caller invokes `KB_DatabaseService.manageDatabaseBackup({action:'export'})` without `backupPath`, it breaks at `fs.ensureDir(undefined)`. Migration to SDK-layer config resolves this without needing a per-server patch.
> 
> **Suggested addition to this ticket's Acceptance Criteria list:**
> - [ ] `backupPath` migrated to SDK-layer config; both `Memory_DatabaseService` and `KB_DatabaseService` dispatchers read from the shared SDK-level default; MC + KB server configs drop their local `backupPath` entries
> 
> Origin Session ID: 5a521819-dc75-4549-888e-fcea818d0401
